questions.question: A company has dozens of nearly identical roles for each project and environment. Which authorization strategy may simplify the design?
answers: ABAC based on tags
Dedicated hosts for each team
S3 Transfer Acceleration
Static passwords in Secrets Manager
questions.shortExplanation: ABAC can reduce role explosion by evaluating shared policy logic against tags.
questions.fullExplanation: ABAC can reduce role explosion by evaluating shared policy logic against tags.
questions.explanation: ABAC can reduce role explosion by evaluating shared policy logic against tags.

questions.question: A company needs full auditability and fine-grained control over the keys used to encrypt an Amazon S3 bucket. Which option is best?
answers: SSE-S3
SSE-KMS with a customer managed key
Public read access
S3 website hosting
questions.shortExplanation: Customer-managed KMS keys provide explicit policy control and better auditability than SSE-S3 alone.
questions.fullExplanation: Customer-managed KMS keys provide explicit policy control and better auditability than SSE-S3 alone.
questions.explanation: Customer-managed KMS keys provide explicit policy control and better auditability than SSE-S3 alone.

questions.question: An application stores database credentials in AWS and requires automatic rotation. Which service should manage those secrets?
answers: AWS Secrets Manager
Amazon Route 53
AWS Cost Explorer
Amazon MQ
questions.shortExplanation: Secrets Manager is built for storing and rotating application secrets such as database credentials.
questions.fullExplanation: Secrets Manager is built for storing and rotating application secrets such as database credentials.
questions.explanation: Secrets Manager is built for storing and rotating application secrets such as database credentials.

questions.question: A team wants to identify buckets that contain personally identifiable information. Which managed service helps most?
answers: Amazon Macie
AWS Shield Standard
AWS Batch
Amazon Route 53 Resolver
questions.shortExplanation: Amazon Macie is the AWS service intended for PII discovery and classification in S3.
questions.fullExplanation: Amazon Macie is the AWS service intended for PII discovery and classification in S3.
questions.explanation: Amazon Macie is the AWS service intended for PII discovery and classification in S3.

questions.question: A security engineer plans to schedule deletion of a KMS key that still protects active data. What is the main risk?
answers: Lower NAT gateway cost
Data may become permanently inaccessible
Improved CloudTrail retention
Automatic S3 replication
questions.shortExplanation: Deleting an active KMS key can make encrypted data unreadable once the waiting period expires.
questions.fullExplanation: Deleting an active KMS key can make encrypted data unreadable once the waiting period expires.
questions.explanation: Deleting an active KMS key can make encrypted data unreadable once the waiting period expires.

questions.question: A team needs to protect data both in transit and at rest. Which pair of controls addresses both goals?
answers: TLS plus encryption at rest
CloudWatch plus budgets
Security groups plus tags
S3 Transfer Acceleration plus Route 53
questions.shortExplanation: Transit and at-rest protection are separate concerns and both must be addressed explicitly.
questions.fullExplanation: Transit and at-rest protection are separate concerns and both must be addressed explicitly.
questions.explanation: Transit and at-rest protection are separate concerns and both must be addressed explicitly.

questions.question: Why might a security team choose a customer managed KMS key over an AWS managed key?
answers: To remove all IAM requirements
To gain explicit policy control and lifecycle ownership
To disable CloudTrail
To avoid encryption costs entirely
questions.shortExplanation: Customer managed keys are preferred when the team needs control over policy, grants, or lifecycle actions.
questions.fullExplanation: Customer managed keys are preferred when the team needs control over policy, grants, or lifecycle actions.
questions.explanation: Customer managed keys are preferred when the team needs control over policy, grants, or lifecycle actions.

questions.question: A data protection program wants to reduce the amount of sensitive data exposed to downstream systems. Which approach helps most?
answers: Tokenization or data minimization
Public snapshots
Wider CIDR ranges
Longer SSH sessions
questions.shortExplanation: Tokenization and minimization reduce how much sensitive data downstream systems ever handle.
questions.fullExplanation: Tokenization and minimization reduce how much sensitive data downstream systems ever handle.
questions.explanation: Tokenization and minimization reduce how much sensitive data downstream systems ever handle.

questions.question: Which statement about backups is correct in a secure AWS design?
answers: Backups can be less protected than primary data
Backups should be protected to the same standard as primary data
Backups do not need encryption
Backups do not need monitoring
questions.shortExplanation: Backups often contain the same sensitive data and should receive equivalent protection.
questions.fullExplanation: Backups often contain the same sensitive data and should receive equivalent protection.
questions.explanation: Backups often contain the same sensitive data and should receive equivalent protection.

questions.question: A team needs cryptographic controls for large-scale data processing with centralized key protection. Which pattern is commonly used?
answers: Envelope encryption
Opening more ports
Disabling TLS
Public AMIs
questions.shortExplanation: Envelope encryption scales cryptographic operations while keeping top-level keys centrally protected.
questions.fullExplanation: Envelope encryption scales cryptographic operations while keeping top-level keys centrally protected.
questions.explanation: Envelope encryption scales cryptographic operations while keeping top-level keys centrally protected.

questions.question: A company retains sensitive records indefinitely without a legal reason. What security issue does this create?
answers: Reduced KMS API usage
Unnecessary long-term exposure
Improved least privilege
Faster scaling
questions.shortExplanation: Unnecessary retention increases breach impact and expands the data that must be defended.
questions.fullExplanation: Unnecessary retention increases breach impact and expands the data that must be defended.
questions.explanation: Unnecessary retention increases breach impact and expands the data that must be defended.

questions.question: A team believes Macie alone is enough to protect sensitive files in S3. What is the best correction?
answers: Macie discovers data but encryption and access controls are still required
Macie replaces KMS entirely
Macie removes IAM requirements
Macie is a firewall service
questions.shortExplanation: Macie helps find and classify sensitive data but does not replace preventive and detective controls around it.
questions.fullExplanation: Macie helps find and classify sensitive data but does not replace preventive and detective controls around it.
questions.explanation: Macie helps find and classify sensitive data but does not replace preventive and detective controls around it.

questions.question: A workload uses KMS heavily and needs to let one service use a key without broadening the key policy for everyone. What should be evaluated?
answers: KMS grants
S3 inventory
Route 53 geolocation
Dedicated hosts
questions.shortExplanation: KMS grants are designed for scoped key usage delegation without large policy changes.
questions.fullExplanation: KMS grants are designed for scoped key usage delegation without large policy changes.
questions.explanation: KMS grants are designed for scoped key usage delegation without large policy changes.

questions.question: A security review shows TLS is terminated at a load balancer and traffic to backend services is plaintext. What is the concern?
answers: The trust boundary may end too early for the sensitivity of the data
The S3 bucket will become public
KMS key rotation will stop
GuardDuty cannot run
questions.shortExplanation: TLS termination location matters because plaintext traffic behind the load balancer may still violate security requirements.
questions.fullExplanation: TLS termination location matters because plaintext traffic behind the load balancer may still violate security requirements.
questions.explanation: TLS termination location matters because plaintext traffic behind the load balancer may still violate security requirements.

questions.question: A compliance team needs to know which principals used a customer-managed key and when. Which AWS capability is most relevant?
answers: KMS usage logged in CloudTrail
S3 lifecycle transitions
Route 53 query logging only
AWS Budgets
questions.shortExplanation: CloudTrail records KMS API activity and supports auditing of key usage and administration.
questions.fullExplanation: CloudTrail records KMS API activity and supports auditing of key usage and administration.
questions.explanation: CloudTrail records KMS API activity and supports auditing of key usage and administration.

questions.question: A company encrypts everything at rest but does not classify its data. What is the main gap?
answers: It may misapply controls and retention because it does not know what is sensitive
Encryption becomes invalid without tags
MFA stops working
S3 replication is disabled
questions.shortExplanation: Classification is needed to prioritize controls, retention, and monitoring based on sensitivity and business impact.
questions.fullExplanation: Classification is needed to prioritize controls, retention, and monitoring based on sensitivity and business impact.
questions.explanation: Classification is needed to prioritize controls, retention, and monitoring based on sensitivity and business impact.

questions.question: An EC2 instance is suspected of compromise and must be isolated quickly while preserving evidence. What is a strong first step?
answers: Terminate the instance immediately
Quarantine the instance by updating security controls and preserve evidence
Disable CloudTrail
Delete all snapshots
questions.shortExplanation: Containment should reduce blast radius without destroying the evidence needed for analysis.
questions.fullExplanation: Containment should reduce blast radius without destroying the evidence needed for analysis.
questions.explanation: Containment should reduce blast radius without destroying the evidence needed for analysis.

questions.question: A team wants to automate quarantine of an EC2 instance when a critical finding is raised. Which pattern is common?

Route 53 changes the domain name
AWS Budgets stops all spending
CloudFront clears the cache
questions.shortExplanation: EventBridge plus Lambda is a common response automation pattern for security findings.
questions.fullExplanation: EventBridge plus Lambda is a common response automation pattern for security findings.
questions.explanation: EventBridge plus Lambda is a common response automation pattern for security findings.

AWS Budgets
Amazon AppStream 2.0

questions.fullExplanation: Amazon Inspector is the managed vulnerability and exposure assessment service for supported workloads.

Amazon Macie
AWS Organizations

questions.fullExplanation: Shield Advanced adds enhanced DDoS protections and response capabilities for public applications.

questions.question: During an investigation, why is preserving CloudTrail logs and related snapshots important?
answers: To reduce IAM complexity
To retain evidence for forensic analysis
To increase Spot capacity
To lower S3 retrieval costs

questions.fullExplanation: Incident response depends on trustworthy logs and preserved system state for timeline reconstruction.
questions.explanation: Incident response depends on trustworthy logs and preserved system state for timeline reconstruction.

questions.question: A security team wants to reduce blast radius in future incidents. Which architectural principle most helps?
answers: Flat networks and shared credentials
Segmentation and least privilege
Bigger instance types
Longer DNS TTLs

questions.question: Why should incident response runbooks be rehearsed regularly?
answers: To make the document longer
To reveal missing access, unclear steps, and weak assumptions
To disable CloudTrail automatically
To increase route table limits
questions.shortExplanation: Exercises expose practical gaps that are usually invisible until a real incident occurs.
questions.fullExplanation: Exercises expose practical gaps that are usually invisible until a real incident occurs.
questions.explanation: Exercises expose practical gaps that are usually invisible until a real incident occurs.

questions.question: After a security incident is contained, what should happen before the event is considered fully resolved?
answers: Delete all logs
Validate recovery and monitor for recurrence
Disable MFA for responders
Remove all metrics
questions.shortExplanation: Recovery should be tested and monitored to ensure compromise has not persisted or recurred.
questions.fullExplanation: Recovery should be tested and monitored to ensure compromise has not persisted or recurred.
questions.explanation: Recovery should be tested and monitored to ensure compromise has not persisted or recurred.

questions.question: A public application needs protection from common web exploits while retaining edge performance. Which combination is most appropriate?

IAM users with passwords
S3 Glacier with Route 53
Amazon SNS with AWS Budgets

questions.fullExplanation: CloudFront with WAF is the standard edge security combination for performance and filtering.

questions.question: What is the primary purpose of a post-incident review?
answers: To increase log ingestion cost
To identify systemic improvements and prevent recurrence
To make backups smaller
To change VPC CIDR blocks
questions.shortExplanation: The goal is to strengthen systems and processes so similar incidents are less likely or less damaging.
questions.fullExplanation: The goal is to strengthen systems and processes so similar incidents are less likely or less damaging.

Route 53 failover records
Savings Plans coverage
Bucket naming conventions

questions.fullExplanation: Ephemeral infrastructure requires centralized logs and automated capture because instance-local evidence disappears quickly.
questions.explanation: Ephemeral infrastructure requires centralized logs and automated capture because instance-local evidence disappears quickly.

questions.question: A quarantine playbook requires manual security group edits and takes twenty minutes to execute. What is the strongest improvement?
answers: Prebuilt automated quarantine workflows
More IAM users
Longer DNS TTLs
Disable Config rules
questions.shortExplanation: Predesigned and automated quarantine reduces response time and inconsistency during active incidents.
questions.fullExplanation: Predesigned and automated quarantine reduces response time and inconsistency during active incidents.
questions.explanation: Predesigned and automated quarantine reduces response time and inconsistency during active incidents.

questions.question: A team restores a system after compromise but does not verify integrity or monitor it closely afterward. What is the main risk?
answers: The compromise could persist or recur unnoticed
KMS charges will double immediately
CloudTrail will stop logging
The VPC will lose subnets
questions.shortExplanation: Recovery without validation can return an attacker or persistence mechanism to service.
questions.fullExplanation: Recovery without validation can return an attacker or persistence mechanism to service.
questions.explanation: Recovery without validation can return an attacker or persistence mechanism to service.

questions.question: A company wants stronger DDoS resilience for a public application. Which answer reflects the best exam mindset?
answers: Treat DDoS protection as an architectural pattern, not only one service
Use only one EC2 instance with detailed monitoring
Disable Auto Scaling to reduce attack surface
Move all traffic through a bastion host
questions.shortExplanation: DDoS resilience depends on edge protection, scaling, routing, and operational response, not one checkbox service.
questions.fullExplanation: DDoS resilience depends on edge protection, scaling, routing, and operational response, not one checkbox service.
questions.explanation: DDoS resilience depends on edge protection, scaling, routing, and operational response, not one checkbox service.

questions.question: An incident response leader says tooling alone will solve future incidents. Which response is most accurate?
answers: Incident response also depends on process, ownership, and architecture
Yes, tools replace runbooks completely
Only more EC2 capacity is needed
CloudTrail makes all runbooks unnecessary
questions.shortExplanation: Effective response is a systems discipline involving people, process, architecture, and tooling together.
questions.fullExplanation: Effective response is a systems discipline involving people, process, architecture, and tooling together.
questions.explanation: Effective response is a systems discipline involving people, process, architecture, and tooling together.
